name: Pyxle
comment: Pyxle .pyxl files: Python server code on top, JSX client code below. Uses begin/end so the embedded grammars' string handling (template literals, triple-quoted strings) takes priority over block switching. Only UNAMBIGUOUS patterns trigger a switch — keywords shared by both languages (return, if, for, while, try) never change the block.

python-block.comment: Python section. Begins on lines that are UNAMBIGUOUSLY Python. Ends (switches to JSX) when a line is UNAMBIGUOUSLY JavaScript/JSX. Using begin/end means source.python handles triple-quoted strings — content inside them won't trigger a block switch.

jsx-block.comment: JSX/JavaScript section. Begins on lines that are UNAMBIGUOUSLY JS. Ends (switches to Python) when a line is UNAMBIGUOUSLY Python. Using begin/end means source.js.jsx handles template literals — content inside backtick strings won't trigger a block switch.
